user_query:
I’m organizing our neighborhood safety campaign and need your help to turn our local files into a brief, accurate status report and cleaner outreach messages. Please work only with the files in the input/ folder and produce two outputs:

1) Create a concise campaign status report at outputs/campaign_status_summary.md that includes:
- Goals: List each policy goal’s short_name and ask from input/policy_goals.yaml.
- Community Feedback Snapshot: From input/resident_feedback.jsonl, report the total counts of support, oppose, and neutral; then list the top three themes by frequency across all entries (break ties alphabetically) with their counts.
- Meeting Highlights: From input/meeting_notes/2026-04-10_meeting.md, summarize key decisions and action items (include owner and deadline), note petition signatures to date, and clearly state the next council meeting date and time.
- Sources: List the input filenames used for each section.
All numbers in this report must be derived from the inputs. Keep the report clear and scannable in plain language.

2) Rewrite the outreach drafts in input/drafts/draft_messages.md and save to outputs/revised_messages.md with these constraints:
- Keep each original heading (e.g., “DRAFT #1: …”) and any Subject line text exactly as-is at the top of its section.
- Under each heading, provide one rewritten message only.
- Tone: respectful, constructive, and non-partisan; remove shouty wording and excessive punctuation.
- Clarity and brevity: plain language; maximum 120 words per message.
- Include exactly one concrete ask that references one short_name from input/policy_goals.yaml verbatim in each rewritten message.
- Do not introduce URLs or new facts not found in the inputs.

Input paths:
- input/policy_goals.yaml
- input/meeting_notes/2026-04-10_meeting.md
- input/resident_feedback.jsonl
- input/drafts/draft_messages.md

Output paths:
- outputs/campaign_status_summary.md
- outputs/revised_messages.md

If anything is unclear, make reasonable, minimal assumptions but do not rely on the web. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
